In [ ]:
import numpy as np
import pandas as pd
import matplotlib.pyplot as plt
import seaborn as sns
In [ ]:
df = pd.read_csv('/content/Churn_Modelling.csv')
df.head()
Out[ ]:
RowNumber CustomerId Surname CreditScore Geography Gender Age Tenure Balance NumOfProducts HasCrCard IsActiveMember EstimatedSalary Churn
0 1 15634602 Hargrave 619 France Female 42 2 0.00 1 1 1 101348.88 1

3 4 15701354 Boni 699 France Female 39 1 0.00 2 0 0 93826.63 0
4 5 15737888 Mitchell 850 Spain Female 43 2 125510.82 1 1 1 79084.10 0

In [ ]:
df1 = df.copy()
In [ ]:
df1.head()
Out[ ]:
RowNumber CustomerId Surname CreditScore Geography Gender Age Tenure Balance NumOfProducts HasCrCard IsActiveMember EstimatedSalary Churn
0 1 15634602 Hargrave 619 France Female 42 2 0.00 1 1 1 101348.88 1
1 2 15647311 Hill 608 Spain Female 41 1 83807.86 1 0 1 112542.58 0
2 3 15619304 Onio 502 France Female 42 8 159660.80 3 1 0 113931.57 1
3 4 15701354 Boni 699 France Female 39 1 0.00 2 0 0 93826.63 0
4 5 15737888 Mitchell 850 Spain Female 43 2 125510.82 1 1 1 79084.10 0
In [ ]:
df1['HasCrCard'] = df1['HasCrCard'].map({0:'No',1:'Yes'})
df1['IsActiveMember'] = df1['IsActiveMember'].map({0:'No',1:'Yes'})
df1['Churn'] = df1['Churn'].replace({0:'No',1:'Yes'})
In [ ]:
df1.head()
Out[ ]:
RowNumber CustomerId Surname CreditScore Geography Gender Age Tenure Balance NumOfProducts HasCrCard IsActiveMember EstimatedSalary Churn
0 1 15634602 Hargrave 619 France Female 42 2 0.00 1 Yes Yes 101348.88 Yes
1 2 15647311 Hill 608 Spain Female 41 1 83807.86 1 No Yes 112542.58 No
2 3 15619304 Onio 502 France Female 42 8 159660.80 3 Yes No 113931.57 Yes
3 4 15701354 Boni 699 France Female 39 1 0.00 2 No No 93826.63 No
4 5 15737888 Mitchell 850 Spain Female 43 2 125510.82 1 Yes Yes 79084.10 No
In [ ]:
sns.boxplot(df1)
Out[ ]:
<Axes: >
In [ ]:
# professional credit score bins
bins = [0, 579, 669, 739, 799, 1000]  # upper bound slightly above 850 for safety
labels = ['Poor', 'Fair', 'Good', 'Very Good', 'Excellent']

df1['CreditScoreGroup'] = pd.cut(
    df1['CreditScore'],
    bins=bins,
    labels=labels,
    right=True,
    include_lowest=True
)
In [ ]:
# professional credit score bins
bins = [0, 12, 19, 34, 59, 100]  # upper bound slightly above 850 for safety
labels = ['Children', 'Teenagers', 'Youth', 'Adult', 'Old Age']

df1['AgeGroup'] = pd.cut(
    df1['Age'],
    bins=bins,
    labels=labels,
    right=True,
    include_lowest=True
)
In [ ]:
df1['NumOfProducts'].unique()
Out[ ]:
array([1, 3, 2, 4])
In [ ]:
df1.head()
Out[ ]:
RowNumber CustomerId Surname CreditScore Geography Gender Age Tenure Balance NumOfProducts HasCrCard IsActiveMember EstimatedSalary Churn CreditScoreGroup AgeGroup
0 1 15634602 Hargrave 619 France Female 42 2 0.00 1 Yes Yes 101348.88 Yes Fair Adult
1 2 15647311 Hill 608 Spain Female 41 1 83807.86 1 No Yes 112542.58 No Fair Adult
2 3 15619304 Onio 502 France Female 42 8 159660.80 3 Yes No 113931.57 Yes Poor Adult
3 4 15701354 Boni 699 France Female 39 1 0.00 2 No No 93826.63 No Good Adult
4 5 15737888 Mitchell 850 Spain Female 43 2 125510.82 1 Yes Yes 79084.10 No Excellent Adult
In [ ]:
bins = [0, 4515, 14005, 10**9]
labels = ["Low Income", "Moderate Income", "High Income"]
df1["EstimatedSalaryGroup"] = pd.cut(
    df1["EstimatedSalary"],
    bins=bins,
    labels=labels,
    right=True,
    include_lowest=True
)

df1.head()
Out[ ]:

       'Gender', 'Age','AgeGroup', 'Tenure', 'Balance', 'NumOfProducts', 'HasCrCard',
       'IsActiveMember', 'EstimatedSalary', 'EstimatedSalaryGroup', 'Churn']]

In [ ]:
creditScore_analysis = df1.pivot_table(index="CreditScoreGroup", columns="Churn", values="CustomerId", aggfunc="count")
creditScore_analysis

/tmp/ipython-input-1531055324.py:1: FutureWarning: The default value of observed=False is deprecated and will change to observed=True in a future version of pandas. Specify observed=False to silence this warning and retain the current behavior
  creditScore_analysis = df1.pivot_table(index="CreditScoreGroup", columns="Churn", values="CustomerId", aggfunc="count")
Out[ ]:
Churn No Yes
CreditScoreGroup
Poor 1842 520
Fair 2646 685
Good 1976 452
Very Good 972 252
Excellent 527 128
In [ ]:
creditscore_total = df1.pivot_table(index="CreditScoreGroup", values="CustomerId", aggfunc="count")
creditscore_total

/tmp/ipython-input-423712351.py:1: FutureWarning: The default value of observed=False is deprecated and will change to observed=True in a future version of pandas. Specify observed=False to silence this warning and retain the current behavior
  creditscore_total = df1.pivot_table(index="CreditScoreGroup", values="CustomerId", aggfunc="count")
Out[ ]:
CustomerId
CreditScoreGroup
Poor 2362
Fair 3331
Good 2428
Very Good 1224
Excellent 655
In [ ]:
creditScore_analysis_percentage = (creditScore_analysis.div(creditscore_total.values, axis=0) * 100).round(2)
creditScore_analysis_percentage
Out[ ]:
Churn No Yes
CreditScoreGroup
Poor 77.98 22.02
Fair 79.44 20.56
Good 81.38 18.62
Very Good 79.41 20.59
Excellent 80.46 19.54

In [ ]:
bins = [0, 3, 6, 20]
labels = ["New", "Established", "Loyal"]
df1["TenureGroup"] = pd.cut(
    df1["Tenure"],
    bins=bins,
    labels=labels,
    right=True,
    include_lowest=True
)

TRAINING THE MODEL¶

In [ ]:
df.head()
Out[ ]:
RowNumber CustomerId Surname CreditScore Geography Gender Age Tenure Balance NumOfProducts HasCrCard IsActiveMember EstimatedSalary Churn
0 1 15634602 Hargrave 619 France Female 42 2 0.00 1 1 1 101348.88 1
1 2 15647311 Hill 608 Spain Female 41 1 83807.86 1 0 1 112542.58 0
2 3 15619304 Onio 502 France Female 42 8 159660.80 3 1 0 113931.57 1
3 4 15701354 Boni 699 France Female 39 1 0.00 2 0 0 93826.63 0
4 5 15737888 Mitchell 850 Spain Female 43 2 125510.82 1 1 1 79084.10 0
In [ ]:
x = df.drop(["RowNumber", "CustomerId", "Surname", "Churn"], axis=1)
y = df["Churn"]
In [ ]:
x.head()
Out[ ]:
CreditScore Geography Gender Age Tenure Balance NumOfProducts HasCrCard IsActiveMember EstimatedSalary
0 619 France Female 42 2 0.00 1 1 1 101348.88
1 608 Spain Female 41 1 83807.86 1 0 1 112542.58
2 502 France Female 42 8 159660.80 3 1 0 113931.57
3 699 France Female 39 1 0.00 2 0 0 93826.63
4 850 Spain Female 43 2 125510.82 1 1 1 79084.10
In [ ]:
y.head()
Out[ ]:
Churn
0 1
1 0
2 1
3 0
4 0

In [ ]:
# Encode the Gender and Geography Column

from sklearn.preprocessing import LabelEncoder
le = LabelEncoder()

x["Gender"] = le.fit_transform(x["Gender"])
x["Geography"] = le.fit_transform(x["Geography"])
In [ ]:
x.head()
Out[ ]:
CreditScore Geography Gender Age Tenure Balance NumOfProducts HasCrCard IsActiveMember EstimatedSalary
0 619 0 0 42 2 0.00 1 1 1 101348.88
1 608 2 0 41 1 83807.86 1 0 1 112542.58
2 502 0 0 42 8 159660.80 3 1 0 113931.57
3 699 0 0 39 1 0.00 2 0 0 93826.63
4 850 2 0 43 2 125510.82 1 1 1 79084.10
In [ ]:
# Split the data into training and testing set

from sklearn.model_selection import train_test_split

x_train, x_test, y_train, y_test = train_test_split(x, y, test_size=0.2, random_state=42)
In [ ]:
# Standerdizing the model

from sklearn.preprocessing import StandardScaler
sc = StandardScaler()
In [ ]:
x_train = sc.fit_transform(x_train)
x_test = sc.transform(x_test)
In [ ]:
x_train
Out[ ]:
array([[ 0.35649971, -0.9055496 ,  0.91324755, ...,  0.64920267,
         0.97481699,  1.36766974],
       [-0.20389777,  0.30164867,  0.91324755, ...,  0.64920267,
         0.97481699,  1.6612541 ],
       [-0.96147213,  1.50884694,  0.91324755, ...,  0.64920267,
        -1.02583358, -0.25280688],
       ...,
       [ 0.86500853, -0.9055496 , -1.09499335, ..., -1.54035103,
        -1.02583358, -0.1427649 ],
       [ 0.15932282, -0.9055496 ,  0.91324755, ...,  0.64920267,
        -1.02583358, -0.05082558],
       [ 0.47065475,  0.30164867,  0.91324755, ...,  0.64920267,
         0.97481699, -0.81456811]])
In [ ]:
x_test
Out[ ]:
array([[-0.57749609,  0.30164867,  0.91324755, ..., -1.54035103,
        -1.02583358, -1.01960511],
       [-0.29729735, -0.9055496 ,  0.91324755, ...,  0.64920267,
         0.97481699,  0.79888291],
       [-0.52560743,  1.50884694, -1.09499335, ...,  0.64920267,
        -1.02583358, -0.72797953],
       ...,
       [ 0.81311987, -0.9055496 , -1.09499335, ...,  0.64920267,
        -1.02583358, -1.16591585],
       [ 0.41876609, -0.9055496 ,  0.91324755, ...,  0.64920267,
        -1.02583358, -0.41163463],
       [-0.24540869,  0.30164867,  0.91324755, ...,  0.64920267,
         0.97481699,  0.12593183]])
In [ ]:
# Training the model using Logistic Regression

from sklearn.linear_model import LogisticRegression
lr = LogisticRegression()
In [ ]:
lr.fit(x_train, y_train)
Out[ ]:
LogisticRegression()
In a Jupyter environment, please rerun this cell to show the HTML representation or trust the notebook.
On GitHub, the HTML representation is unable to render, please try loading this page with nbviewer.org.
LogisticRegression()
In [ ]:
y_pred = lr.predict(x_test)
y_pred
Out[ ]:
array([0, 0, 0, ..., 0, 0, 0])
In [ ]:
# Evaluate the model

from sklearn.metrics import accuracy_score, confusion_matrix, classification_report

ac = print("Accuracy_score: ", accuracy_score(y_test, y_pred))
cm = print(confusion_matrix(y_test, y_pred))
cr = print(classification_report(y_test, y_pred))

Accuracy_score:  0.8155
[[1559   48]
 [ 321   72]]
              precision    recall  f1-score   support

           0       0.83      0.97      0.89      1607
           1       0.60      0.18      0.28       393

    accuracy                           0.82      2000
   macro avg       0.71      0.58      0.59      2000
weighted avg       0.78      0.82      0.77      2000
In [ ]:
# Training the model uaing RandomForestClassifier

from sklearn.ensemble import RandomForestClassifier
rf = RandomForestClassifier(n_estimators=100, random_state=42)
In [ ]:
rf.fit(x_train, y_train)
Out[ ]:
RandomForestClassifier(random_state=42)
In a Jupyter environment, please rerun this cell to show the HTML representation or trust the notebook.
On GitHub, the HTML representation is unable to render, please try loading this page with nbviewer.org.
RandomForestClassifier(random_state=42)
In [ ]:
rf_pred = rf.predict(x_test)

rf_df = pd.DataFrame({"Actual": y_test, "Predicted": rf_pred})
rf_df
Out[ ]:
Actual Predicted
6252 0 0
4684 0 0
1731 0 0
4742 0 0
4521 0 0
... ... ...
6412 1 1
8285 0 0
7853 1 1
1095 1 0
6929 1 0

2000 rows × 2 columns

In [ ]:
# Evaluate the model

print("Accuracy_score: ",accuracy_score(y_test, rf_pred))
print(confusion_matrix(y_test, rf_pred))
print(classification_report(y_test, rf_pred))

Accuracy_score:  0.8645
[[1545   62]
 [ 209  184]]
              precision    recall  f1-score   support

           0       0.88      0.96      0.92      1607
           1       0.75      0.47      0.58       393

    accuracy                           0.86      2000
   macro avg       0.81      0.71      0.75      2000
weighted avg       0.85      0.86      0.85      2000
In [ ]:
# Training the model using KNearestNeighbor

from sklearn.neighbors import KNeighborsClassifier
knn = KNeighborsClassifier(n_neighbors=5)
In [ ]:
knn.fit(x_train, y_train)
Out[ ]:
KNeighborsClassifier()
In a Jupyter environment, please rerun this cell to show the HTML representation or trust the notebook.
On GitHub, the HTML representation is unable to render, please try loading this page with nbviewer.org.
KNeighborsClassifier()
In [ ]:
knn_pred = knn.predict(x_test)
knn_df = pd.DataFrame({"Actual": y_test, "Predicted": knn_pred})

           0       0.86      0.95      0.90      1607
           1       0.63      0.39      0.48       393

    accuracy                           0.84      2000
   macro avg       0.75      0.67      0.69      2000
weighted avg       0.82      0.84      0.82      2000
In [ ]:
# Training the model using decision tree

from sklearn.tree import DecisionTreeClassifier
dt = DecisionTreeClassifier(random_state=42)
In [ ]:
dt.fit(x_train,y_train)
Out[ ]:
DecisionTreeClassifier(random_state=42)
In a Jupyter environment, please rerun this cell to show the HTML representation or trust the notebook.
On GitHub, the HTML representation is unable to render, please try loading this page with nbviewer.org.
DecisionTreeClassifier(random_state=42)
In [ ]:
dt_pred = dt.predict(x_test)
dt_df = pd.DataFrame({"Actual": y_test, "Predicted": dt_pred})
dt_df

2000 rows × 2 columns

In [ ]:
print("Accuracy_score: ",accuracy_score(y_test, dt_pred))
print(confusion_matrix(y_test, dt_pred))
print(classification_report(y_test, dt_pred))

The best algorithm to train this model is RandomForestClassifier because it has an accuracy of 86%¶
